---
title: "0999_functions"
author: "Puvvula"
date: "2023-08-02"
output: pdf_document
---

#function to create imputed datasets - leftcenslognorm imputation - 20 sets
```{r}
generate_imputed_datasets <- function(...) {
  datasets <- list(...)
  imputed_datasets <- list()

  for (i in seq_along(datasets)) {
    dataset <- datasets[[i]]
    variable_name <- deparse(substitute(dataset))  # Get the name of the dataframe object

    imputed_dataset <- dataset |>
      mice(m = 50, 
           method = c(rep("", 40), "leftcenslognorm"), 
           maxit = 50, #setting to default
           seed = 2023,
           lod=c(rep(NA, 40), 0.1)) |>
      clean_names()

    imputed_datasets[[i]] <- imputed_dataset
    assign(paste0("imp_", variable_name), imputed_dataset, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  }

  return(imputed_datasets)
}
```

# Built GEE and pooling using examples from UCLA: 
#https://stats.oarc.ucla.edu/r/faq/how-do-i-perform-multiple-imputation-using-predictive-mean-matching-in-r/

#geeglm parallel
```{r}
runGeeglmParallel<- function(datasets, outcomes, covariates, outputFolder) {
  # Set the number of cores to use for parallel processing
  num_cores <- detectCores()
  
  # Create a cluster for parallel processing
  cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
  
  # Parallelize the outer loop using mclapply
  mclapply(datasets, function(dataset) {
    # Extract the last part of the input data name
    exposure <- tail(strsplit(dataset, "_")[[1]], 1)
  
    # Load the dataset
    data <- get(dataset)
    
    # Set exposure values censored as unmeasured or NR-interference to missing
    data$exposure[data$exposure > 5000] <- NA
  
    # Iterate over each outcome
    for (outcome in outcomes) {
      # Construct the formula string for geeglm analysis
      formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ log(get(exposure))*visit +", paste(covariates, collapse = " + "))
      
      # Perform geeglm analysis
      fit <- with(data, geeglm(formula(formula_str),
                               id = participant_id, 
                               family = "gaussian", 
                               corstr = "independence"))
    
      # Save the output to the provided folder in RDA format
      outputName <- paste(outputFolder, "/", dataset, "_", outcome, "_", exposure, ".rda", sep = "")
      save(fit, file = outputName)
    }
  }, mc.cores = num_cores)
  
  # Stop the cluster
  stopCluster(cl)
}
```

#extract output from gee parallel
```{r}
extractGEEParallel<- function(input_folder, output_folder) {
  # Get a list of RDA files in the input folder
  rda_files <- list.files(input_folder, pattern = "\\.rda$", full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Initialize an empty dataframe to store the results
  data <- data.frame()
  
  # Set the number of cores to use for parallel processing
  num_cores <- detectCores()
  
  # Create a cluster for parallel processing
  cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
  
  # Parallelize the loop using mclapply
  results <- mclapply(rda_files, function(file) {
    # Load the RDA file
    load(file)
    
    # Perform operations and extract required rows
    extracted_data <- as_tibble(tidy(pool(fit), conf.int = TRUE)) |>
      filter(grepl("log\\(get\\(exposure\\)\\)", term)) #results for exposure and exposure-visit interactions
    
    # Get the filename without path or extension
    filename <- str_remove(basename(file), "\\.[^.]+$")
    
    # Trim the filename to remove the "imp_dat_" prefix
    trimmed_filename <- str_remove(filename, "^imp_dat_")
    
    # Split the filename into exposure and outcome variables
    split_strings <- str_split(trimmed_filename, "_")
    exposure <- split_strings[[1]][1]
    outcome <- paste(split_strings[[1]][2:(length(split_strings[[1]]) - 1)], collapse = "_")
    
    # Add the exposure and outcome variables
    extracted_data$exposure <- exposure
    extracted_data$outcome <- outcome
    
    return(extracted_data)
  }, mc.cores = num_cores)
  
  # Stop the cluster
  stopCluster(cl)
  
  # Bind the extracted data from all processes
  data <- bind_rows(results)
  
  # Export the dataframe as a CSV to the output folder
  output_file <- file.path(output_folder, "output.csv")
  write_csv(data, output_file)
}

```


drop NR due to interference or unmeasured
#| data[[last_variable_name]] == 7777.000
```{r}
drop_NR_interference<- function(data, last_variable_name) {
  # Identify rows where the last variable is equal to 9999.000 (interference)
  rows_to_drop <- which(data[[last_variable_name]] == 9999.000 )
  
  # Get the participant_id corresponding to these rows
  participant_ids_to_drop <- data$participant_id[rows_to_drop]
  
  # Drop all observations with the identified participant_id
  data <- data[!(data$participant_id %in% participant_ids_to_drop), , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Return the modified data frame
  return(data)
}

```

#summary function
```{r}
summarize_by_category <- function(data, continuous_vars, categorical_vars) {
  # Initialize an empty list to store results
  result_list <- list()
  
  # Loop over each categorical variable
  for (cat_var in categorical_vars) {
    # Loop over each continuous variable
    for (cont_var in continuous_vars) {
      # Summarize mean and standard deviation by the categorical variable
      summary_df <- data |> 
        group_by(.data[[cat_var]]) |> 
        summarize(
          summary = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", 
                            mean(.data[[cont_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 
                            sd(.data[[cont_var]], na.rm = TRUE)),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) |> 
        mutate(
          variable = cont_var,
          category = paste(cat_var, as.character(.data[[cat_var]]), sep = "-")
        )
      
      # Append the result to the list
      result_list[[paste0(cat_var, "_", cont_var)]] <- summary_df
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all results into a single dataframe
  result_df <- bind_rows(result_list) |> 
    select(variable, category, summary)
  
  return(result_df)
}
```

#single imputation
```{r}
fLOD <- function(test.result, meanlog2, sdlog2){ ##Create a new function, fLOD, which needs 3 inputs: the LOD value (represented by labres$test.result in FLAGGED rows); the mean (meanlog2); the sd (sdlog2)
  impute <- rtruncnorm(1, b=log2(test.result), mean=meanlog2, sd=sdlog2) #the function will then run the rtruncnorm command given the inputs (eg. b= upper limit = log2(LOD), mean, and sd) 
  return(impute) 
}

impute <- function(data, dist_data, name, short_name) {
  # Convert name to character if not already
  name <- as.character(name)
  
  # Extract mean and standard deviation for the given test name
  meanlog2 <- dist_data$meanlog2[dist_data$test.name == name]
  sdlog2 <- dist_data$sdlog2[dist_data$test.name == name]
  
  # Impute values and create the updated dataset
  data_imputed <- data |> 
    mutate(
      log2_imputed = ifelse(
        flg_lod == 1,
        apply(data, 1, function(x) fLOD(result, meanlog2, sdlog2)),
        log2_result
      ),
      untrans_res = 2^(log2_imputed)  # Un-transform results
    ) |> 
    select(participant_id, flg_lod, untrans_res)
  
  # Rename columns for clarity
  colnames(data_imputed)[2] <- paste0(short_name, ".flag")
  colnames(data_imputed)[3] <- paste0(short_name, ".res")
  
  return(data_imputed)
}


process_combination <- function(analyte_code, visit) {
  filtered_data <- ope_exp_raw |>
    filter(analyte_code == !!analyte_code, visit == !!visit)
  
  # Generate the name dynamically
  name <- paste0(analyte_code, ".t", visit)
  
  # Check if `meanlog2` and `sdlog2` are available in `dist_data`
  meanlog2 <- LODmeansd.all$meanlog2[LODmeansd.all$test.name == name]
  sdlog2 <- LODmeansd.all$sdlog2[LODmeansd.all$test.name == name]
  
  if (length(meanlog2) == 0 || length(sdlog2) == 0) {
    warning(paste("Missing meanlog2 or sdlog2 for:", name))
    return(NULL) # Skip this combination
  }
  
  # Apply the imputation
  imputed_data <- impute(
    data = filtered_data,
    dist_data = LODmeansd.all,
    name = name,
    short_name = name
  )
  
  # Extract `analyte_code` and `visit` from column names
  analyte_result_var <- paste0(analyte_code, ".t", visit, ".res")
  flg_lod_var <- paste0(analyte_code, ".t", visit, ".flag")
  
  # Rename columns for consistency
  imputed_data <- imputed_data |>
    rename(
      flg_lod = all_of(flg_lod_var),
      analyte_result = all_of(analyte_result_var)
    ) |>
    mutate(
      analyte_code = analyte_code,
      visit = visit
    ) |>
    select(participant_id, analyte_code, visit, analyte_result, flg_lod)
  
  return(imputed_data)
}

```

#aft imputation : https://github.com/ml5977/LOD_accommodation/blob/main/Mixture_LOD_example_code.R 
#need Log2 transformed exposure data as input
```{r}
library(survival)
library(tmvtnorm)

perform_aft_imputation <- function(data, predictor_vars, z_vars, ind_vars, covariate_vars) {
  # Copy input data
  aft.data <- data
  
  # Transform covariates to exp scale
  aft.data[,covariate_vars] <- exp(aft.data[,covariate_vars])
  
  # Get dimensions
  lod.n <- length(z_vars)
  n <- nrow(aft.data)
  
  # Initialize matrices
  aft.coef.mat <- matrix(NA, ncol=length(predictor_vars) + 1, nrow=lod.n)
  aft.resi.mat <- matrix(NA, ncol=lod.n, nrow=n)
  aft.mean.mat <- matrix(NA, ncol=lod.n, nrow=n)
  
  # AFT model fitting
  for(i in 1:length(z_vars)) {
    TT <- -log(aft.data[,z_vars[i]])
    delta <- aft.data[,ind_vars[i]]
    formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(exp(TT), delta) ~", paste(predictor_vars, collapse="+")))
    aft.reg <- survreg(formula, dist="lognormal", data=aft.data)
    aft.coef.mat[i,] <- aft.reg$coefficients
    aft.resi.mat[,i] <- TT-as.matrix(cbind(1, aft.data[,predictor_vars]))%*%matrix(aft.reg$coefficients)
    aft.mean.mat[,i] <- as.matrix(cbind(1, aft.data[,predictor_vars]))%*%matrix(aft.reg$coefficients)
  }
  
  # Estimate covariance matrix
  aft.resi.mat2 <- aft.resi.mat
  aft.resi.mat2 <- ifelse(aft.data[,ind_vars]==1, aft.resi.mat2, NA)
  sigma <- cov(aft.resi.mat2, use="pairwise.complete.obs")
  
  # Imputation
  imputed.res.mat <- aft.resi.mat2
  delta.mat <- aft.data[,ind_vars]
  
  for(k in 1:n){
    ind.delta <- delta.mat[k,]
    ind.res <- aft.resi.mat[k,]
    obs.d <- which(ind.delta==1)
    mis.d <- which(ind.delta==0)
    
    if(sum(ind.delta)==0){
      mu.cond <- rep(0, lod.n)
      cov.cond <- sigma
      tmu.cond <- mtmvnorm(mean=c(mu.cond), sigma=cov.cond, lower=ind.res[mis.d])$tmean
      imputed.res.mat[k,mis.d] <- tmu.cond
    }else if(sum(ind.delta)!=lod.n){
      mu.cond <- sigma[mis.d, obs.d]%*%solve(sigma[obs.d, obs.d])%*%(ind.res[obs.d])
      cov.cond <- sigma[mis.d, mis.d] - sigma[mis.d, obs.d]%*%solve(sigma[obs.d, obs.d])%*%sigma[obs.d, mis.d]
      tmu.cond <- mtmvnorm(mean=c(mu.cond), sigma=cov.cond, lower=ind.res[mis.d])$tmean
      tmu.cond <- ifelse(is.na(tmu.cond)==TRUE, ind.res[mis.d], tmu.cond)
      imputed.res.mat[k,mis.d] <- tmu.cond
    }
  }
  
  imputed.Z <- exp(-(aft.mean.mat + imputed.res.mat))
  aft.data[,z_vars] <- imputed.Z
  aft.data[,covariate_vars] <- log(aft.data[,covariate_vars])
  
  return(aft.data)
}
```

#qgc
```{r}
qgcomp_func <- function(outcomes, data, output_folder, chemicals, covariates,
                        include_sex = TRUE, q, b, seed) {
    
    sex_levels <- if (include_sex) c("all", "Female", "Male") else "all"
    
    for (sex_level in sex_levels) {
        if (sex_level == "all") {
            sex_data <- data
            sex_formula <- "sex"
        } else {
            sex_data <- subset(data, sex == sex_level)
            sex_formula <- ""
        }
        
        filename_prefix <- sex_level
        
        for (outcome in outcomes) {
            formula <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~",
                                        paste(chemicals, collapse = " + "), "+",
                                        sex_formula, "+",
                                        paste(covariates, collapse = " + ")))
            
            nb <- qgcomp.noboot(formula, expnms = mixture, data = sex_data, family = gaussian(), q = q)
            boot <- qgcomp.boot(formula, expnms = mixture, data = sex_data, family = gaussian(), q = q, B = b, seed = seed)
            save(nb, file = paste0(output_folder, "/", "nb_", filename_prefix, "_", outcome, ".rda"))
            save(boot, file = paste0(output_folder, "/", "boot_", filename_prefix, "_", outcome, ".rda"))
        }
    }
}
```

```{r}
get_gcomp_estimates<- function(results_folder) {
  # get the file names in the folder
  file_names <- list.files(results_folder)

  # create empty data frame to store the results
  results <- data.frame(file = character(),
                        estimate = numeric(),
                        lower_ci = numeric(),
                        upper_ci = numeric(),
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  #loops through all .rda files and extract estimates, CI & p-values
  for (file_name in file_names) {
    # load the object from the file
    load(file.path(results_folder, file_name))
    # extract the object name using grep or grepl
    object_name <- ls()[grep("^boot|^nb", ls())]
    # extract the required information using the summary function and the object name
    estimate <- get(object_name)$coef["psi1"]
    CI <- get(object_name)$ci
    lower_ci <- CI[1]
    upper_ci <- CI[2]
    p_value <- get(object_name)$pval[2]
    # split file name by "_" and remove ".rda"
    file_parts <- strsplit(gsub("\\.rda", "", file_name), "_")[[1]]
    # assign parts to variables
    part1 <- file_parts[1]
    part2 <- file_parts[2]
    part3 <- file_name
    # print out the values
    cat("File:", file_name, "\n")
    cat("  Estimate:", estimate, "\n")
    cat("  Lower CI:", lower_ci, "\n")
    cat("  Upper CI:", upper_ci, "\n")
    cat("  p-value:", p_value, "\n")
    cat("  Part 1:", part1, "\n")
    cat("  Part 2:", part2, "\n")
    cat("  Part 3:", part3, "\n")
    # add the results to the data frame
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(boot_strp = part1,
                                         gender = part2,
                                         filename = part3,
                                         estimate = estimate,
                                         lower_ci = lower_ci,
                                         upper_ci = upper_ci,
                                         p_value = p_value,
                                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
    # remove the object from the R environment to avoid conflicts with other objects (all objects saved as nb or boot)
    rm(list = object_name)
  }

  return(results)
}
```

#qgcomp weights
```{r}
extract_weights <- function(folder_location) {
  # get list of files that start with "nb"
  nb_files <- list.files(path = folder_location, pattern = "^nb.*\\.rda$", full.names = TRUE)

  # create an empty list to store results
  results_list <- list()

  # loop through each nb file
  for (file in nb_files) {
    # load data from file
    load(file)

    # extract neg and pos weights
    neg <- as.data.frame(nb$neg.weights) |>
      rename(weight = `nb$neg.weights`) |>
      rownames_to_column("chemical") |>
      mutate(direction = "neg")

    pos <- as.data.frame(nb$pos.weights) |>
      rename(weight = `nb$pos.weights`) |>
      rownames_to_column("chemical") |>
      mutate(direction = "pos")

    weights_df <- bind_rows(neg, pos)

    # extract file name without path and .rda extension using regular expression
    filename <- sub(".*/(.*)\\.rda", "\\1", file)

    # Add "file_name" as a variable to weights_df
    weights_df$filename <- filename

    # Extract psi values and add them as rows
    if (is.list(nb$psi)) {
      psi1 <- nb$psi$psi1
    } else {
      psi1 <- NA
    }

    psi_df <- data.frame(chemical = "neg.psi", weight = nb$neg.psi, direction = "neg_psi", filename = filename) |>
      rbind(data.frame(chemical = "pos.psi", weight = nb$pos.psi, direction = "pos_psi", filename = filename)) |>
      rbind(data.frame(chemical = "psi1", weight = nb$psi, direction = "psi1", filename = filename))

    # Append psi_df to weights_df
    combined_df <- bind_rows(weights_df, psi_df)

    # Append combined_df to results_list
    results_list[[filename]] <- combined_df
  }

  # Combine all results into a single dataframe
  results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

  return(results_df)
}
```

```{r}
lm_func <- function(dependent_vars, independent_vars, data, covariates, include_sex = TRUE, include_cohort = FALSE, conf_level = 0.95) {
  # Create empty lists to store results
  dependent_list <- list()
  independent_list <- list()
  sex_level_list <- list()
  coef_list <- list()
  p_value_list <- list()
  ci_lower_list <- list()
  ci_upper_list <- list()
  
  if ("sex" %in% colnames(data)) {
    sex_present <- TRUE
  } else {
    sex_present <- FALSE
    include_sex <- FALSE
  }
  
  sex_levels <- if (include_sex) c("all", "Female", "Male") else "all"
  
  for (sex_level in sex_levels) {
    if (sex_level == "all" || !sex_present) {
      sex_data <- data
      # Only include sex as covariate when analyzing both sexes together
      if (sex_level == "all" && sex_present) {
        all_covariates <- c(covariates, "sex")
      } else {
        all_covariates <- covariates
      }
    } else {
      sex_data <- subset(data, sex == sex_level)
      all_covariates <- covariates  # Don't include sex as a covariate for sex-specific analyses
    }
    
    # Check for factor variables with only one level in this subset
    valid_covariates <- c()
    for (cov in all_covariates) {
      if (is.factor(sex_data[[cov]])) {
        # Drop levels that don't appear in this subset
        sex_data[[cov]] <- droplevels(sex_data[[cov]])
        if (length(levels(sex_data[[cov]])) >= 2) {
          valid_covariates <- c(valid_covariates, cov)
        } else {
          warning(paste("Covariate", cov, "dropped for", sex_level, "as it has only one level"))
        }
      } else {
        valid_covariates <- c(valid_covariates, cov)
      }
    }
    
    # Loop through all combinations of dependent and independent variables
    for (i in 1:length(dependent_vars)) {
      for (j in 1:length(independent_vars)) {
        # Create the covariates formula dynamically based on the valid covariates
        if (length(valid_covariates) > 0) {
          covariate_formula <- paste(valid_covariates, collapse = " + ")
          formula_str <- paste(dependent_vars[i], "~", independent_vars[j], "+", covariate_formula)
        } else {
          formula_str <- paste(dependent_vars[i], "~", independent_vars[j])
        }
        
        # Run linear regression with dynamic covariates
        formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
        
        tryCatch({
          model <- lm(formula, sex_data)
          
          # Calculate confidence interval
          ci <- confint(model, parm = independent_vars[j], level = conf_level)
          
          # Store results in lists
          dependent_list[[length(dependent_list) + 1]] <- dependent_vars[i]
          independent_list[[length(independent_list) + 1]] <- independent_vars[j]
          sex_level_list[[length(sex_level_list) + 1]] <- sex_level
          coef_list[[length(coef_list) + 1]] <- coef(model)[independent_vars[j]]
          p_value_list[[length(p_value_list) + 1]] <- summary(model)$coefficients[independent_vars[j], 4]
          ci_lower_list[[length(ci_lower_list) + 1]] <- ci[1]
          ci_upper_list[[length(ci_upper_list) + 1]] <- ci[2]
          
        }, error = function(e) {
          warning(paste("Error in model for", dependent_vars[i], "~", independent_vars[j], "with sex =", sex_level, ":", e$message))
        })
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Create a dataframe with results
  if (length(dependent_list) > 0) {
    results <- data.frame(
      dependent_variable = unlist(dependent_list),
      independent_variable = unlist(independent_list),
      sex_level = unlist(sex_level_list),
      coefficient = unlist(coef_list),
      p_value = unlist(p_value_list),
      ci_lower = unlist(ci_lower_list),
      ci_upper = unlist(ci_upper_list)
    )
    return(results)
  } else {
    warning("No models were successfully fit")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

```
